procedural_text:
Here are the procedural steps derived from the article:

1. Recognize BISSNEX completing key market expansion actions and gaining recognition from the U.S. federal government and financial systems.

2. Note the U.S. demonstrating rising friendliness toward the crypto industry, supporting cryptocurrency taxation as property following Bitcoin and Ethereum ETF proposals.

3. Observe The International Organization of Securities Commissions (IOSCO) releasing the Consultation Report on Policy Recommendations for Crypto-Asset and Digital Asset Markets, advancing global crypto regulation standards.

4. Record BISSNEX obtaining the Money Services Business (MSB) license from the U.S. Financial Crimes Enforcement Network, demonstrating compliance with AML and KYC standards.

5. Confirm U.S. states California and New York recognizing cryptocurrencies as legal digital assets.

6. Establish BISSNEX strategically headquartering in New York, leveraging its stringent regulatory framework and financial resources.

7. Announce BISSNEX expanding its compliance team, recruiting specialists in cryptocurrency regulations.

8. Direct BISSNEX's compliance team to ensure operations comply with all local and international regulations.

9. Confirm www.bdctp.co Data News supplying data and news, with Barchart Quotes supplying stock quotes, delayed at least 20 minutes.

1. Contact Toptal Expert and receive response within a few several hours
2. Discuss project requirements with Expert to confirm suitability
3. Estimate development costs at some thousand pounds based on project scope
4. Collect valuable phrases and integrate them into project specification document
5. Hire Blessed developer carefully to avoid negative freelancer experiences
6. Assign developer to deliver project scope, execution, Charge, and Management document
7. Implement Main Data object changes ensuring proper propagation across threads
8. Enable NSZombies in Xcode to debug exc_bad_access errors effectively
9. Retain NSZombies to keep released objects alive as "zombies" during debugging
10. Review Core Facts objects fetched from persistent storage on separate threads
11. Begin engagement with iOS iPad developer during trial period of up to 2 months
12. Confirm engagement success within trial period before proceeding
13. Contact Invoice department to bill for time upon satisfactory results
14. Coordinate with Main Data and NSZombies tools through Charge and Core Facts systems
15. Extend iPad development engagement as long as required after successful trial completion

1. Identify Joblane as a feature-rich interactive job portal allowing job seekers and employers to find matches.
2. Use Joblane's knowledge-sharing tools to source qualified programmer candidates.
3. Contact Toptal to begin the hiring process for expert-level programming help.
4. Allow Toptal to match candidates within 24 hours, as demonstrated by the Scala developer search.
5. Designate Toptal as the 1st resource for expert-level technical assistance.
6. Review Claudio's credentials as an Accredited SharePoint specialist with Microsoft client experience.
7. Verify Claudio's expertise in maximizing security, performance, and value for Microsoft environments.
8. Evaluate Feel's expectations and team dynamics before finalizing any hiring decisions.
9. Consider candidates who have spent decades in the industry and have established trusted contacts.
10. Assign developers based on specific project technicalities required by each client.
11. Engage Adam to architect and develop database and BI solutions within competitive timelines.
12. Ensure BI solutions remain efficient, maintainable, and free of unnecessary complexity.
13. Confirm Scala or other framework requirements before sourcing through Toptal or alternative platforms.
14. Finalize hiring decision and allow new developer to immediately begin contributing to the project.

1. Receive statement from Agus Justianto, acting director general of PHL at KLHK, on Thursday regarding 2023 sustainable forest management performance.

2. Confirm Justianto's announcement that multiple performance indicators in 2023 exceeded targets.

3. Record planting and enrichment activities covering 667,780 hectares of PBPH area in 2023, reaching 156 percent above the 428 thousand hectare target.

4. Document log production reaching 58.13 million cubic meters in 2023, achieving 101.9 percent of the 57 million cubic meter target.

5. Categorize log production sources: 89.01 percent from plantation forests, 7.37 percent from natural forests, 1.59 percent from BUMN Perhutani, and 2.03 percent from non-forestry.

6. Process logs as raw materials into woodworking products, plywood, wood chips, wood panels, and pulp.

7. Record non-timber forest product production in 2023 reaching 820.2 thousand tons, equivalent to 193 percent of the 425 thousand ton target.

8. Document forestry partnerships providing legal access to 20,643 hectares in 2023, achieving 137.6 percent of the 15 thousand hectare target.

9. Formalize forestry partnerships through signing of MoU and Cooperation Agreement Text between communities and PBPH.

10. Implement MUK across 14,078 hectares of PBPH areas in seven provinces and 28,203 hectares across 12 KPH units this year.

11. Expand MUK model implementation to 64 KPH units covering 12,210 hectares across 14 provinces.

12. Receive confirmation from APHI chairperson Indroyono Soesilo that improving business climate supports sustainable forest management performance.

13. Direct PBPH to accelerate MUK implementation and diversify forest product industry products going forward.

14. Arrange representatives from 14 countries to learn peat management in Riau.

1. Enter Embark Technology and Applied Intuition, Inc. into Agreement and Plan of Merger on May 25, 2023.

2. Merge Azara Merger Sub, Inc. with and into Embark, with Embark continuing as surviving corporation and wholly owned subsidiary of Applied.

3. File putative class action complaint in Court by Marceil A. Hanshew against Embark and Embark's Board of Directors on June 26, 2023, captioned Marceil A. Hanshew v. Embark Technology, Inc. et al.

4. Allege complaint contains false and misleading statements in preliminary proxy statement filed with SEC on June 16, 2023, and seek preliminary injunction to enjoin Merger.

5. File definitive Proxy statement on Schedule 14A with SEC by Embark on June 26, 2023.

6. File amended complaint by Plaintiffs David L. Hanshew and Marceil A. Hanshew on June 29, 2023, captioned David L. Hanshew in His Capacity as Attorney-in-Fact for Marceil A. Hanshew and Marceil A. Hanshew v. Embark Technology, Inc. et al.

7. Negotiate agreement between Plaintiffs and Defendants to resolve the Action, requiring Embark to make additional disclosures.

8. File Amendment No. 1 to the Proxy with SEC by Embark on July 10, 2023, containing agreed additional disclosures.

9. Dismiss the Action voluntarily by Plaintiffs as moot on August 1, 2023.

10. Enter stipulated order by Court on August 1, 2023, dismissing the Action with prejudice as to Plaintiffs and without prejudice as to putative class members.

11. Retain jurisdiction by Court solely to adjudicate Plaintiffs' counsel's anticipated application for attorneys' fees and expenses related to July 10 Amendment disclosures.

12. Agree by Applied, as successor in interest of Embark, to pay $114,000 to Plaintiffs' counsel for attorneys' fees and expenses in full satisfaction of claims in the Action.

13. Enter order by Court on December 21, 2023, closing case, subject to Applied filing affidavit confirming issuance of notice from MOUNTAIN VIEW, Calif.

14. Issue notice by Applied confirming settlement terms as of Dec. 28, 2023.

1. Identify the time of year when families spend days together at home doing jigsaw puzzles.
2. Recognize the problem of finding the right-sized surface for puzzle pieces.
3. Complete nine jigsaws with piece counts ranging from nine to 2,000, as conducted by Madeleine Bonsma-Fisher and Kent Bonsma-Fisher.
4. Lay out and measure pieces for each puzzle, placing them as naturally as possible.
5. Avoid artificially positioning pieces close together during measurement.
6. Painstakingly complete each jigsaw puzzle.
7. Calculate the length and width of each assembled puzzle picture.
8. Determine that unassembled pieces take up approximately 1.7 times the finished puzzle size.
9. Apply a simple theory to predict the area needed for pieces regardless of piece count.
10. Confirm the best table size is just under twice the size of the assembled puzzle.
11. Note Madeleine Bonsma-Fisher of the University of Toronto described the theory-data agreement as the most incredible she had seen in over a decade.
12. Publish findings on open-access website arXiv.
13. Report research through New Scientist.

1. Apply Katherine Bennell-Pegg to astronaut candidate position, competing against more than 22,000 applicants for the role at the European Astronaut Centre.

2. Select Katherine Bennell-Pegg, Australian Space Agency employee and former Director of Space Technology, as an astronaut candidate.

3. Relocate Ms Bennell-Pegg and her family to Germany for the intensive year-long training process.

4. Begin Katherine Bennell-Pegg's training at the European Astronaut Centre in Germany, incorporating rigorous physical training and intensive study.

5. Complete first half of Ms Bennell-Pegg's year-long training program, reaching the halfway milestone.

6. Travel Ms Bennell-Pegg to Houston in early December for specialised training with NASA.

7. Meet Ms Bennell-Pegg with Australian Ambassador Kevin Rudd in Washington DC during the United States visit.

8. Continue The next six months of training, incorporating intense exercise, sea survival, space station robotic arm simulations, and centrifuge training.

9. Study Ms Bennell-Pegg medical procedures, including research on Earth conditions such as osteoporosis and muscle degradation accelerated in weightlessness.

10. Complete Ms Bennell-Pegg's full training program, positioning her to become Australia's first female astronaut trained under the Australian Space Agency.

1. Discover Billionaire Brain Wave program designed to activate the Theta wave for attracting wealth.

2. Understand Dave Mitchell created Billionaire Brain Wave after struggling financially and accidentally discovering audio technology.

3. Begin listening to the Billionaire Brain Wave system daily to accelerate goal achievement.

4. Continue usage for a full thirty day period before evaluating results, as individual timelines vary.

5. Note some users experience results within the first day, while others require months to activate results.

6. Recognize three additional sound waves within Billionaire Brain Wave that boost Theta waves for manifesting success.

7. Activate brain waves to eliminate beta wave interference blocking financial achievement.

8. Tap into intuition through Billionaire Brain Wave, allowing deepest desires to manifest faster.

9. Awaken daily using program guidance to achieve tranquility and limitless possibilities.

10. Purchase Billionaire Brain Wave at Value price of $39, ensuring budget-friendly accessibility for everyone.

11. Avoid fake Billionaire Brain Wave imitations, which provide no benefit despite high cost.

12. Consult licensed healthcare provider before changes, noting FDA has not evaluated claims made.

13. Attract prosperity from multiple sources, removing debt, raising savings, and experiencing financial freedom through consistent Billionaire Brain Wave practice.

1. Launch Centre for Ecocities seven-year International Ecological Footprint Learning Lab project in collaboration with York University and the University of Iceland, funded by SSHRC.

2. Begin developing Urban Consumption Guidebook for Cities with C40 Cities, OneEarth Living, and Hot or Cool Institute for spring launch.

3. Secure funding from the Real Estate Foundation to develop BC-specific Policy and Planning Guide addressing Consumption-Based Emission Inventories priorities while advancing equity.

4. Develop archetype tool to simplify creation of Consumption-Based Emission Inventories and Ecological Footprint assessments for communities across BC.

5. Complete Ecological Footprint assessments and CBEIs using ecoCity Footprint Tool developed by Dr. Jennie Moore for the City of Ottawa, District of Saanich, and other municipalities.

6. Deliver presentation in March - Cora Hallsworth presents to Climate Caucus encouraging Canadian cities to consider consumption-based emissions.

7. Have Christina Olsen represent the Centre presenting to CMHC Expert Group on Housing regarding BC municipalities reducing greenhouse gas emissions.

8. Host Ecocity Webinar on Sustainable Lifestyles in May exploring local government climate action approaches.

9. Send Christina Olsen to serve on Academic Committee at Ecocity World Summit in June in London.

10. Have Christina Olsen lead June workshop on closing sustainability and equity gap, including video from the District of Saanich.

11. Have Christina Olsen participate in September panel on solving over-consumption conundrum at Fully Charged event.

12. Coordinate October Ecocity Peer Network Zoom meeting with George Heyman, Minister of Environment and Climate Change Strategy for BC.

13. Have Cora Hallsworth represent the Centre at the Victoria stop of the Regenerate Cascadia Bioregional Activation Tour panel in October.

14. Co-host November workshop at Zero Waste Conference in Vancouver on Building Circular Culture for Climate Action.

15. Have Dr. Jennie Moore publish paper on sustainability at urban scale with colleagues from Université du Québec and University of British Columbia in 2023.

16. Interview Dr. Moore on CKNW 980 radio discussing socially just and ecologically sustainable cities.

17. Feature Dr. Moore's comments in Vancouver Sun regarding urban ecological pressures and loss of six Stanley Parks of natural habitat.

18. Integrate Ecological Footprint as core metric within the District of Saanich's Official Community Plan, currently in final approval stages.

19. Implement reporting requirements for embodied carbon impacts for new Part 3 buildings in the City of Vancouver, with reduction requirements effective January 2025.

1. Identify Nasa genus plants (Loasaceae family) hidden in tropical Andes, previously thought extinct for over 100 years.

2. Recognize challenge of collecting Nasa plants due to their stinging leaves, rarity, short durations, and region-specific locations limiting herbarium collections.

3. Utilize iNaturalist platform to access geo-referenced occurrence records and photographic biodiversity data submitted by community scientists.

4. Locate 2019 photograph on iNaturalist documenting Nasa colanii, originally recorded only once in 1978, found in Peru's Cordillera de Colán National Sanctuary at 2,605 meters elevation.

5. Identify Nasa ferox in 2022 through iNaturalist photographs, noting its scientific description was formulated in 2000 despite approximately 130 years of absence from reports.

6. Confirm small surviving population of N. ferox sheltering in rock crevices and shrub bases within its known range.

7. Rediscover Nasa humboldtiana subspecies humboldtiana after 162 years in conserved montane Andean forest in Chimborazo, Ecuador.

8. Uncover Nasa hastata and Nasa solaria, both indigenous to the Peruvian Department of Lima, still thriving through iNaturalist photographs, including photos taken by a relative of one author.

9. Compile findings and publish completed research in PhytoKeys journal.

10. Issue media statement through Tilo Henning of the Leibniz Center for Agricultural Landscape Research (ZALF) emphasizing biodiversity documentation's role in conservation efforts.
